sn35.232:1.1: Once, Ven. Sāriputta and Ven. Mahā Koṭṭhita were staying near Vārāṇasī in the Deer Park at Isipatana.
sn35.232:1.2: Then Ven. Mahā Koṭṭhita, in the evening, left his seclusion and went to Ven. Sāriputta. On arrival, he exchanged courteous greetings with him.
sn35.232:1.3: After an exchange of friendly greetings & courtesies, he sat to one side. As he was sitting there, he said to Ven. Sāriputta,
sn35.232:2.1: “Now tell me, friend Sāriputta, is the eye the fetter of forms, or are forms the fetter of the eye? Is the ear… Is the nose…
sn35.232:2.2: Is the tongue… Is the body…
sn35.232:2.3: Is the intellect the fetter of ideas, or are ideas the fetter of the intellect?”
sn35.232:3.1: “No, my friend. The eye is not the fetter of forms, nor are forms the fetter of the eye.
sn35.232:3.2: Whatever desire-passion arises in dependence on the two of them: That is the fetter there. The ear is not the fetter of sounds… The nose is not the fetter of aromas…
sn35.232:3.3: The tongue is not the fetter of flavors… The body is not the fetter of tactile sensations…
sn35.232:3.5: The intellect is not the fetter of ideas, nor are ideas the fetter of the intellect.
sn35.232:3.6: Whatever desire-passion arises in dependence on the two of them: That is the fetter there.
sn35.232:4.1: “Suppose that a black ox and a white ox were joined with a single collar or yoke.
sn35.232:4.3: ‘The black ox is the fetter of the white ox, the white ox is the fetter of the black’—speaking this way, would he be speaking rightly?”
sn35.232:4.5: The black ox is not the fetter of the white ox, nor is the white ox the fetter of the black.
sn35.232:4.6: The single collar or yoke by which they are joined:
sn35.232:5.2: “In the same way, the eye is not the fetter of forms, nor are forms the fetter of the eye. Whatever desire-passion arises in dependence on the two of them: That is the fetter there. The ear is not the fetter of sounds… The nose is not the fetter of aromas…
sn35.232:5.3: The tongue is not the fetter of flavors… The body is not the fetter of tactile sensations…
sn35.232:5.4: The intellect is not the fetter of ideas, nor are ideas the fetter of the intellect.
sn35.232:5.5: Whatever desire-passion arises in dependence on the two of them: That is the fetter there.
sn35.232:6.1: “If the eye were the fetter of forms, or if forms were the fetter of the eye,
sn35.232:6.2: then this holy life for the right ending of stress & suffering would not be proclaimed.
sn35.232:6.3: But because whatever desire-passion arises in dependence on the two of them is the fetter there, that is why this holy life for the right ending of stress & suffering is proclaimed. “If the ear were the fetter… “If the nose were the fetter…
sn35.232:7.1: “If the tongue were the fetter… “If the body were the fetter…
sn35.232:8.1: “If the intellect were the fetter of ideas, or if ideas were the fetter of the intellect,
sn35.232:8.2: then this holy life for the right ending of stress & suffering would not be proclaimed.
sn35.232:8.3: But because whatever desire-passion arises in dependence on the two of them is the fetter there, that is why this holy life for the right ending of stress & suffering is proclaimed.
sn35.232:9.1: “And through this line of reasoning one may know how the eye is not the fetter of forms, nor are forms the fetter of the eye,
sn35.232:9.2: but whatever desire-passion arises in dependence on the two of them: That is the fetter there. The ear is not the fetter of sounds… The nose is not the fetter of aromas…
sn35.232:9.3: The tongue is not the fetter of flavors… The body is not the fetter of tactile sensations…
sn35.232:9.4: The intellect is not the fetter of ideas, nor are ideas the fetter of the intellect,
sn35.232:9.5: but whatever desire-passion arises in dependence on the two of them: That is the fetter there.
sn35.232:10.1: There is an eye in the Blessed One.
sn35.232:10.2: The Blessed One sees forms with the eye.
sn35.232:10.3: There is no desire or passion in the Blessed One.
sn35.232:10.4: The Blessed One is well released in mind.
sn35.232:10.5: “There is an ear in the Blessed One…
sn35.232:10.9: “There is a nose in the Blessed One…
sn35.232:10.13: “There is a tongue in the Blessed One…
sn35.232:10.17: “There is a body in the Blessed One…
sn35.232:10.21: “There is an intellect in the Blessed One.
sn35.232:10.22: The Blessed One knows ideas with the intellect.
sn35.232:10.23: There is no desire or passion in the Blessed One.
sn35.232:10.24: The Blessed One is well released in mind.
sn35.232:11.1: “It is through this line of reasoning that one may know how the eye is not the fetter of forms, nor are forms the fetter of the eye,
sn35.232:11.2: but whatever desire-passion arises in dependence on the two of them: That is the fetter there.
sn35.232:11.3: The ear is not the fetter of sounds…
sn35.232:11.4: The nose is not the fetter of aromas…
sn35.232:11.5: The tongue is not the fetter of flavors…
sn35.232:11.7: The body is not the fetter of tactile sensations…
sn35.232:11.8: The intellect is not the fetter of ideas, nor are ideas the fetter of the intellect,
sn35.232:11.9: but whatever desire-passion arises in dependence on the two of them: That is the fetter there.”
